methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Pano AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Sage Geosystems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $400M

private.name: Reverion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $310M

private.name: CarbonCapture Inc.
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M

private.name: Cemvita
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
EnerVenue, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Yi Cui, has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of nine firms spanning one public peer (LNZA) and eight private companies in the Climate & Energy sector at equivalent or proximate stages.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $400M total raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $760M. Peer-Median analysis examines five same-sector, same-stage companies—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—producing a median valuation of $310M and a range of $120M to $500M. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity in business model maturity and technology readiness within the climate-tech cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $264M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $535M. This wide band reflects material uncertainty around stage-advancement velocity and peer comparability. Key variables include the pace at which EnerVenue achieves commercialization milestones relative to peers and the degree to which its capital intensity and technology differentiation support premium positioning within the peer set. The Stage-Progression method anchors to capital deployment; Peer-Median grounds valuation in observable market transactions, though limited public comp coverage constrains precision.
